 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-17i8","title":"P4.4: Local Preview Server","description":"# Local Preview Server

**Parent Phase:** Phase 4: Wizard & Deployment
**Depends On:** P4.1 (Interactive Wizard)
**Duration:** 1-2 days

## Goal

Implement a local HTTP server for previewing exported archives before deployment, with proper COOP/COEP headers.

## Technical Approach

### CLI Command

cass pages --preview <DIR>
cass pages --preview ./cass-pages-export/site

### Features

1. Serve Static Files
   - All files from site/ directory
   - Proper MIME types

2. Add COOP/COEP Headers
   - Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin
   - Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp

3. Live URL Display
   Preview available at: http://localhost:8080
   Press Ctrl+C to stop

4. Auto-Open Browser (optional)
   --open flag to launch default browser

### Implementation Options

Option A: Use tiny_http crate
- Lightweight, simple
- ~100 lines of code

Option B: Use axum/actix
- More features
- Heavier dependencies

Recommend Option A for minimal footprint.

### MIME Type Handling

.html  -> text/html
.js    -> application/javascript
.wasm  -> application/wasm
.json  -> application/json
.css   -> text/css
.bin   -> application/octet-stream

### Exit Criteria

1. Server starts on available port
2. COOP/COEP headers set
3. All file types served correctly
4. Graceful shutdown on Ctrl+C
5. Error message if port in use","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:38:14.255861418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T06:01:36.971235356Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T06:01:36.971235356Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of coding_agent_session_search-regb","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-17i8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-9cby","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:38:21.587148347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-17i8","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-rzst","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T03:34:09.023113537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1bqi","title":"[Feature] Performance Metrics Collection","description":"## Feature: Performance Metrics Collection

Emit performance metrics during E2E tests for regression detection.

### Metrics to Collect
| Metric | Unit | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `indexing_duration_ms` | ms | Time to index test corpus |
| `search_latency_p50_ms` | ms | Median search query time |
| `search_latency_p99_ms` | ms | 99th percentile search time |
| `memory_peak_kb` | KB | Peak memory usage |
| `index_size_bytes` | bytes | Size of search index |
| `files_processed` | count | Number of files indexed |
| `queries_per_second` | qps | Search throughput |

### Implementation
```rust
// In tests/util/e2e_log.rs
pub fn emit_metric(name: &str, value: f64, unit: &str) -> Result<()> {
    let event = json\!({
        "ts": Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
        "event": "metric",
        "name": name,
        "value": value,
        "unit": unit,
        "run_id": run_id()
    });
    writeln\!(log_file(), "{}", event)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Usage
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
indexer.index_all()?;
emit_metric("indexing_duration_ms", start.elapsed().as_millis() as f64, "ms")?;
```

### Baseline Tracking
- Store baselines in `test-results/baselines.json`
- Alert if metric exceeds baseline by >20%
- Update baseline after confirmed improvements

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `emit_metric` function in e2e_log
- [ ] 7 metrics collected per E2E run
- [ ] Baseline comparison implemented
- [ ] Alert on regression >20%
- [ ] Metrics visible in JSONL output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:59.502864770Z","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:22:42.175833582Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:22:42.175703911Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1bqi","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ohe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:26:10.915148705Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1d42ae1e","title":"Cursor-Based Pagination","description":"# Cursor-Based Pagination

## Problem Statement
Offset-based pagination (`--offset N`) has issues:
- Results can shift if index changes between pages
- Hard to track position reliably
- Inefficient for large offsets

## Proposed Solution
Add cursor-based pagination:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --limit 10
# Returns: {"next_cursor": "abc123", "hits": [...]}

cass search "error" --json --limit 10 --cursor "abc123"
# Returns next page
```

## Design Decisions

### Cursor Encoding
Cursor contains:
- Query hash (to validate same query)
- Last seen sort key (score + id)
- Expiration timestamp

Encoded as base64 for URL safety.

### Cursor Expiration
Cursors expire after 1 hour to prevent stale state issues.

### Fallback
If cursor is invalid/expired, return error with hint to restart.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--cursor` parameter for continuing pagination
- [ ] `next_cursor` in response when more results available
- [ ] Cursor validates query hasn't changed
- [ ] Expired cursors return clear error
- [ ] Works with all output formats

## Effort Estimate
Medium - 3-4 hours. Requires cursor encoding and validation logic.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.002460810Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:04:40.516945Z","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","title":"T2: Mock Elimination - Replace Transitional Mocks","description":"# Epic: Eliminate Transitional Mocks

## Goal
Remove all transitional mock patterns from the codebase, replacing them with real implementations or test fixtures.

## Current Transitional Mocks (from no_mock_allowlist.json)
1. tests/connector_claude.rs - mock_claude variable
2. tests/fs_errors.rs - mock_claude variable
3. tests/pages_bundle.rs - fake_config

## Review Deadline
All transitional mocks scheduled for removal by 2026-03-26

## Approach
- Create real fixture directories from actual session data
- Use ConversationFixtureBuilder for deterministic test data
- Replace fake configs with real PagesConfig instances

## Dependencies
- Blocked by T1 (need unit tests before removing mocks)
- Referenced in downstream task bd-1dfc","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:19:57.403736141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637335096Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637268994Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3fbl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:22:10.386929338Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
